questions.question: Explain the term biodiversity.
questions.answer: Biodiversity refers to the variety of plant and animal species found in a particular region.

questions.question: Why is India considered a mega biodiversity country?
questions.answer: Because it has a very large variety of plant and animal species.

questions.question: Why do evergreen forests have multilayered vegetation?
questions.answer: Because warm and wet climate supports dense plant growth.

questions.question: Why do deciduous forests shed leaves in summer?
questions.answer: To conserve moisture during dry seasons.

questions.question: Why do plants in thorn forests have long roots?
questions.answer: To reach deep underground water.

questions.question: How does altitude affect vegetation in mountainous regions?
questions.answer: Vegetation changes with altitude due to temperature differences.

questions.question: Why are mangrove roots partly submerged in water?
questions.answer: Because they grow in tidal coastal areas.

questions.question: Why are mangrove forests important for coastal ecosystems?
questions.answer: They prevent coastal erosion and protect against storms.

questions.question: Why are forests important for ecological balance?
questions.answer: They regulate climate and support biodiversity.

questions.question: Why are elephants found mainly in dense forests?
questions.answer: Because these forests provide sufficient food and water.

questions.question: Why are wildlife sanctuaries created?
questions.answer: To protect animals and their habitats.

questions.question: Why is hunting harmful to biodiversity?
questions.answer: It reduces animal populations and threatens species survival.

questions.question: Why is deforestation a threat to wildlife?
questions.answer: It destroys natural habitats.

questions.question: Why are national parks important?
questions.answer: They conserve wildlife and natural ecosystems.

questions.question: Why are biosphere reserves important for research?
questions.answer: They protect ecosystems and support scientific study.

questions.question: Why are medicinal plants valuable to humans?
questions.answer: They are used to treat various diseases.

questions.question: Why are insects important for ecosystems?
questions.answer: They help in pollination and biological control.

questions.question: Why are migratory birds attracted to wetlands?
questions.answer: Because wetlands provide food and breeding areas.

questions.question: Why is conservation necessary for future generations?
questions.answer: It preserves biodiversity and natural resources.

questions.question: Why do alpine trees become shorter near the snow line?
questions.answer: Due to harsh climatic conditions.

questions.question: Why is the Sundarbans important for wildlife conservation?
questions.answer: It is the habitat of the Royal Bengal Tiger.

questions.question: Why are rivers and lakes important for aquatic wildlife?
questions.answer: They provide habitat for fish, turtles and crocodiles.

questions.question: Why should pollution be controlled to protect biodiversity?
questions.answer: Because pollution harms ecosystems and living organisms.

questions.question: Why do governments establish zoological gardens?
questions.answer: To protect endangered animals and promote awareness.

questions.question: Why is it important to stop indiscriminate cutting of forests?
questions.answer: Because it helps maintain ecological balance.
